div1.entryFree: Shrive, to hear at confession and absolve: I had rather he should s. me than wive me, Merch. I, 2, 144. he --s this woman to her smock, H6A I, 2, 119. your honour hath no --ing work in hand, R3 III, 2, 116. there she shall be --d and married, Rom. II, 4, 194. not --ing time allowed, Hml. V, 2, 47. With of, == to hear the confession and absolve of: I'll s. you of a thousand idle pranks, Err. II, 2, 210.
